COUNCIL MEETING
A G E N D A
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 5:00 P.m. - 8:00 P.m.
Council Chamber, City Hall
4949 Canada Way, Burnaby, BC
Code of Conduct - Glossary of Terms
 
1.
CALL TO ORDER
 
2.
TERRITORIAL ACKNOWLEDGEMENT
 
3.
ANNOUNCEMENTS
 

4.

4.1
Open Council Meeting Agenda for March 10, 2026
 
5.
ADOPTION OF MINUTES

COTW Meeting Minutes February 23, 2026

DELEGATION: Solo Karaoke Ltd. - LLA #25-12 -

102-6462 Kingsway

Cong Ren, Solo Karaoke Ltd.

recommendation to support the subject liquor primary licence.

COMMENTS ON PROPOSED METRO 2050
AMENDMENT OPTIONS IN RESPONSE TO
REQUEST BY THE MAYORS OF SURREY, LANGLEY
TOWNSHIP AND DELTA

2026 - Proposed Metro 2050 Amendmen Options
2026-03-10.pdf

- Proposed Metro 2050 Amendment Options 2026-03-10.pdf

comment letter to the Metro Vancouver Board conveying
concerns about the four proposed Metro 2050 amendment
options.

4111 NORLAND AVENUE (COMMUNITY SAFETY

2026-03-10.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Sketches 1 and 2 - 4111 Norland Avenue
2026-03-10.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval to grant a Statutory Right-
of-Way over a portion of 4111 Norland Avenue to permit the

GOVERNMENT DEVELOPMENT APPROVALS

1.GA-Application to UBCM LGDAP 2026 Grant Funding

UBCM Local Government Development Approvals funding
program.

CA - IT TICKETING SOFTWARE LICENSES
[image] 
1.CA - IT Ticketing Software Lincenses 2026-03-10.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA)
for IT Ticketing Software Licences.
8.
COMMITTEE REPORTS
 
8.1
ECC - COMMUNITY GRANT POLICY AND
COMMUNITY GRANT APPLICATION
AMENDMENTS
[image] 
1.ECC - Community Grant Policy and Community Grant
Application Amendments 2026.03.10.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Draft Community Grant Policy Revised
2026.03.10.pdf
3.Attachment 2 - Draft Community Grant Application Revised
2026.03.10 .pdf

8.2
PRCC - DRAFT PRC PLAN
[image]

2.Attachment 1 - Draft Making Life Better PRC Plan
2026.03.10.pdf

Better: Parks, Recreation and Culture (PRC) Plan.

PRCC - TOURISM BURNABY: REQUEST FOR
SUPPORT FOR 2028 CANADIAN URBAN FOREST

[image]

ON TABLE

NOVEMBER 2025) AND ANNUAL PERFORMANCE

3.Attachment 2 - APP Quarterly Report (Jul - Sept 2025)

10.
INFORMATION REPORTS
 
None.
11.
BYLAWS
 
11.1
FIRST, SECOND AND THIRD READING
 
11.1.1

#14805 - Burnaby Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle
Charging Meter Bylaw 2019, Amendment Bylaw No. 1,
2026
[image] 
1.14805 Burnaby Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging
Meter Bylaw 2019 Amendment Bylaw No. 1 2026.pdf
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle
Charging Meter Bylaw 2019 (public EV network)
(Item 8.3., FMC Report, Council February 24, 2026)
11.1.2
#14806 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw,
Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026
[image] 
1.14806 Burnaby Consolidated Fees Charges Bylaw
Amendment Bylaw No. 2 2026.pdf
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges
Bylaw (public EV network)
(Item 8.3., FMC Report, Council February 24, 2026)
11.1.3
#14807 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009,
Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026
[image] 
1.14807 Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amend Bylaw
No. 2, 2026 Burnaby Parking Meter_Electric Charging
Meter.pdf
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement

Bylaw 2009 (public EV network)
(Item 8.3., FMC Report, Council February 24, 2026)
11.2
THIRD READING AND FINAL ADOPTION
 
11.2.1
#14792 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw
No. 2, 2026 - Text Amendment
[image] 
1.14792. Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 Amendment No. 2, 2026
New Zoning Districts final (3 and FA).pdf
Purpose: to propose amendments to the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw
to establish five new zoning districts and related changes to
implement the Burnaby 2050 OCP land use framework
(Item 7.2., Administrative Report, January 13, 2026, and Item
7.1., Administrative Report, February 10, 2026)
11.3
 FINAL ADOPTION
 
11.3.1
#14744 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw
No. 8, 2025 - REZ #24-20 (7135 Walker Ave. and 7244
Arcola St.)
[image] 
1.14744 Rez 24-20 (7135 Walker and 7244 Arcola).ACD 2025

FINAL (FA).pdf
2.REZ 24-20 Final Adoption Memo 2026-03-10.pdf
Purpose: to establish development guidelines for the
construction of a 50-storey residential rental tower with an
adjoining 6-storey community hub inclusive of an auditorium,
retail space, community serving uses (such as a food bank), and
office space. The development proposal includes approximately
480 purpose-built rental units, including approximately 96 non-
market rental units
(Item 7.6., Administrative Report, Council April 22, 2025)
Memorandum - General Manager Planning & Development -
March 10, 2026
11.3.2
#14763 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw
No. 15, 2025 - REZ #25-05 (3944 and 3942 Still Creek Ave)
[image] 
1.14763 Rez 25-05 (3944 and 3942 Still Creek).ACD 2025
(FA).pdf
2.REZ 25-05 Final Adoption Memo 2026-03-10.pdf
Purpose: to amend previous rezoning of the subject lands
pursuant to REZ #22-24 (Bylaw No. 14591), which received
Final Adoption on January 28, 2025 and which permitted the
construction of a five-storey enclosed self-storage facility with
two levels of underground parking. The applicant is now
proposing to remove one parking level and all self-storage uses
from underground parkade, revise vehicular access, and elevate
the building's flood construction level, resulting in a six-storey
self-storage building

(Item 7.6., Administrative Report, Council August 26, 2025)
Memorandum - General Manager Planning & Development -
March 10, 2026
11.3.3
#14782 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw
No. 19, 2025 - REZ #25-15 (5889 Barker Avenue)
[image] 
1.14782 Rez 25-15.ACD 2025 (FA).pdf
2.REZ 25-15 Final Adoption Memo 2026-03-10.pdf
Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands
pursuant to REZ #17-40 (Bylaw No. 14181), which received
Final Adoption of November 8, 2021, and which permitted the
construction of a 43-storey residential strata apartment tower
and a six-storey non-market rental building with underground
parking. The proposed amendment seeks to permit an
additional six storeys of market rental housing above the
approved non-market rental building, resulting in a 12-storey
rental building with a mix of rental rates
(Item 8.11., PDC Report, Council October 28, 2025)
Memorandum - General Manager Planning & Development -
March 10, 2026
11.3.4
#14798 - Burnaby Business Licence Bylaw 2017,
Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
[image] 
1.14798.Burnaby Business Licence Bylaw 2017, Amendment

Bylaw No.1 2026 (FA).pdf
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Business Licence Bylaw 2017 (bear
spray regulations)
(Item 7.4., Administrative Report, Council February 24, 2026)
11.3.5
#14799 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009,
Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
[image] 
1.14799. Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amend
Bylaw No. 1, 2026 Regulating Sale of Bear Spray (FA).pdf
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement
Bylaw 2009 (bear spray regulations)
(Item 7.4., Administrative Report, Council February 24, 2026)
11.3.6
#14800 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw,
Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
[image] 
1.14800.Burnaby Consolidated Fees Charges Bylaw
Amendment Bylaw No. 1 2026 (FA).pdf
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges
Bylaw (plumbing fees)
(Item 7.2., Administrative Report, Council February 24, 2026)
11.3.7
#14801 - Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024, Amendment

Bylaw No. 1, 2026
[image] 
1.14801.Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024 Amendment Bylaw
No.1 2026 (FA).pdf
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024 (Terms of
Reference for Advisory Body)
(Item 7.3., Administrative Report, Council February 10, 2026)
12.
CORRESPONDENCE
 
No items of correspondence were received for Council's
decision. All other items of correspondence were published at
the Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions
package dated March 10, 2026.
13.
OTHER BUSINESS 
 
13.1
CHANGE: Acting Mayor 2026 Schedule
 
Recommendation:
THAT the Acting Mayor 2026 Schedule be AMENDED to

appoint Councillor Lee as Acting Mayor for the month of March
2026, and Councillor Gu as Acting Mayor for the month of
September 2026.
13.2
RESCHEDULING: PDC meetings in 2026
 
Recommendation:
THAT the Planning and Development Committee meeting
scheduled to be held on Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 2 pm in
the Council Chamber be RESCHEDULED to Tuesday, March
31, 2026 at 2 pm in the Council Chamber;
THAT the Planning and Development Committee meeting
scheduled to be held on Wednesday, May 6, 2026 at 2 pm in
the Council Chamber be RESCHEDULED to Monday, May 4,
2026 at 10 am in the Council Chamber;
THAT the Planning and Development Committee meeting
scheduled to be held on Wednesday, July 8, 2026 at 2 pm in
the Council Chamber be RESCHEDULED to Monday, July 6,
2026 at 10 am in the Council Chamber;
THAT the Planning and Development Committee meeting
scheduled to be held on Wednesday, September 2, 2026 at 2
pm in the Council Chamber be RESCHEDULED to Monday,
August 31, 2026 at 2 pm in the Council Chamber; and

THAT the Planning and Development Committee meeting
scheduled to be held on Wednesday, October 7, 2026 at 2 pm
in the Council Chamber be RESCHEDULED to Monday,
October 5, 2026, at 10 am in the Council Chamber.
14.
RELEASE OF CLOSED MEETING
DECISIONS
 
14.1
Resolution No. C2026 - 023
[image] 
1.Attachment 1 - PDC - Determining TAP Eligibility
2026-03-10.pdf
THAT staff be directed to apply the criteria, as outlined in
Attachment 1 of this report (Option 1: Maintain Original Eligibility
Date), to determine tenant eligibility for new rezoning applications,
in respect of development sites with an in-stream rezoning
application; and
THAT the recommendation and Attachment 1 of the report be
released to the Open Council meeting on March 10, 2026.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY 
15.
ADJOURNMENT

briefs.title: Solo Karaoke Liquor Licence Application Receives Delegation
briefs.summary: A delegation has been made regarding Solo Karaoke Ltd.'s liquor licence application. This means someone has been given the authority to act on behalf of a government body in reviewing the application.

briefs.title: Solo Karaoke Liquor Licence Investigation Update
briefs.summary: This is an update on the investigation and recommendation regarding the liquor primary license for Solo Karaoke at 102-6462 Kingsway.

briefs.title: Surrey, Langley, Delta Seek Input on Metro Vancouver's Development Plan
briefs.summary: Surrey, Langley, and Delta are asking for feedback on proposed changes to Metro Vancouver's regional development plan, called Metro 2050. This plan guides how the region will grow and develop over the next few decades. The cities will then send a comment letter to the Metro Vancouver Board.
briefs.action_required: Review the Metro 2050 plan and provide comments to your city.

briefs.title: BC Hydro Right-of-Way on Norland Ave Approved: What Landowners Need to Know
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved granting BC Hydro a statutory right-of-way (legal right to use land) at 4111 Norland Avenue. This gives BC Hydro the right to access the property for things like maintaining power lines. This change likely affects the property owner at this address.
briefs.action_required: Review the terms of the Statutory Right-of-Way agreement with BC Hydro.

briefs.title: Apply Now: UBCM Grant Funding for Local Government Development Approvals Program
briefs.summary: Local governments in BC can apply for a grant from the Union of British Columbia Municipalities (UBCM). The grant supports the Development Approvals Program. This program aims to improve local government processes for development approvals.
briefs.action_required: Consider applying for the 2026 UBCM grant funding.

briefs.title: City Seeks Approval for IT Ticketing Software Licenses Contract
briefs.summary: The city council is reviewing a contract for IT ticketing software licenses. This software helps manage and track IT support requests. Council approval is needed before the contract can be finalized.

briefs.title: Community Grant Policy Changes Proposed
briefs.summary: Your municipality is considering changes to its Community Grant Policy and how to apply. These grants provide financial support to organizations for projects that benefit the community. The proposed changes may affect eligibility criteria, application requirements, and funding priorities.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed changes to see if they affect your organization's grant eligibility.

briefs.title: Tourism Burnaby Seeks Council Support for 2028 Urban Forest Conference Bid
briefs.summary: Tourism Burnaby wants Burnaby City Council to approve their bid to host the 2028 Canadian Urban Forest Conference. If approved, this could bring more tourists and business to Burnaby.

briefs.title: Off-Street Parking Payments May Change; Rezoning Possible
briefs.summary: A city council is considering using money from a parking reserve fund. They may also rezone areas. This could change how developers provide off-street parking. Instead of building parking spaces, they might be able to pay a fee.
briefs.action_required: Monitor council decisions about parking and zoning changes.

briefs.title: Burnaby Amends Parking and Electric Vehicle Charging Bylaw
briefs.summary: Burnaby updated its parking meter and electric vehicle (EV) charging bylaw. The change affects how parking and EV charging are managed in the city.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated bylaw to understand changes to parking and EV charging rules.

briefs.title: Burnaby Amends Fees for Public EV Charging Network
briefs.summary: Burnaby updated its Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw to change fees for using the public electric vehicle (EV) charging network. This bylaw outlines the costs for residents and businesses using public EV chargers in Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Check the new fee structure if you use Burnaby's public EV charging network.

briefs.title: Burnaby Amends Bylaw Notice Enforcement for Public EV Network
briefs.summary: Burnaby updated its bylaw enforcement rules. The change affects how the city handles violations related to its public electric vehicle (EV) charging network. This includes things like parking violations at charging stations.
briefs.action_required: Review parking rules for public EV charging stations in Burnaby.

briefs.title: Burnaby Adds Five New Zoning Districts: What Businesses Need to Know
briefs.summary: Burnaby has created five new zoning districts by amending the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw. These new zones will likely change what types of businesses can operate in certain areas. Check the updated zoning maps to see if your business is affected.
briefs.action_required: Check Burnaby's updated zoning maps to see if your business is affected.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation

briefs.title: Vancouver rezoning: Development guidelines set for Walker Ave rental tower
briefs.summary: Vancouver has new development guidelines for a 50-story rental tower at 7135 Walker Ave. This change is to guide the construction and design of the building.

briefs.title: Burnaby Amends Zoning Bylaw for Still Creek Self-Storage Facility
briefs.summary: Burnaby changed its zoning rules for a self-storage building at 3944 Still Creek Ave. The change affects a previous rezoning approval.

briefs.title: Burnaby: Zoning Amended to Allow More Rental Housing at 5889 Barker Ave
briefs.summary: Burnaby changed its zoning rules for 5889 Barker Avenue. The change allows for more market rental housing to be built at that location.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated zoning bylaw if you own property nearby.

briefs.title: Burnaby Amends Business Licence Bylaw to Regulate Bear Spray
briefs.summary: Burnaby updated its business licence bylaw to include rules about bear spray. The amendment aims to ensure businesses handle and store bear spray safely. This change helps protect the public and prevent misuse.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated bylaw and ensure your business complies with bear spray regulations.

briefs.title: Burnaby Updates Bylaw Enforcement for Bear Spray Regulations
briefs.summary: Burnaby has updated its bylaw enforcement rules. The update affects the rules around bear spray. This change is to Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009.
briefs.action_required: Review updated regulations regarding bear spray and bylaw enforcement in Burnaby.

briefs.title: Burnaby Amends Plumbing Fees Bylaw
briefs.summary: Burnaby updated its Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw to change plumbing fees. This bylaw sets the fees for various services and permits in Burnaby. The amendment changes the amount you'll pay for plumbing-related permits and services.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated fee schedule to understand new plumbing costs.

briefs.title: Burnaby Advisory Body Terms Updated in Procedure Bylaw
briefs.summary: Burnaby updated its Procedure Bylaw to change the terms of reference for advisory bodies. These bodies give advice to the city on different topics. The changes affect how these groups work.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated Procedure Bylaw to understand changes to advisory body terms.

briefs.title: Rezoning Applications: Tenant Assistance Policy (TAP) Eligibility Criteria
briefs.summary: When a property is being considered for rezoning (changing the permitted uses of land), city staff will use specific criteria to decide if tenants living there are eligible for support under the Tenant Assistance Policy (TAP). TAP provides resources to help tenants who may be displaced due to rezoning.
briefs.action_required: Understand how TAP eligibility is determined for rezoning applications.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Construction & Development
